Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "external security audit" or "audit findings" or "Blue Peak" (or similar) using search_emails to find the two Blue Peak Security interim audit findings emails from Megan Holt (mholt@bluepeaksecurity.com) to Denise Holloway on Tue Apr 7 2026 at 07:40 and 07:49 ET, identifying three findings: VPN sessions authenticated with Raj Anand's credentials from an out-of-state IP during overnight/off-hours windows, access into the production LOS database inconsistent with Raj's stated support scope, and recurring borrower data exports observed on a roughly weekly cadence over the last six weeks.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "owner review" or "audit access" or "governance" (or similar) using search_emails or get_thread to find Grace Yamamoto's email to Robert Calloway on Tue Apr 7 2026 at 08:50 ET, subject "Need owner review today on audit access issue", containing Grace's written governance self-disclosure that she approved broader access for Raj Anand than he should have had when he came on for infrastructure support and did not tighten it back down.
OE 3: Search emails with a query related to "Raj" and "audit follow-up" or "stolen" or "laptop" (or similar) using search_emails to find Raj Anand's reply to Grace Yamamoto on Tue Apr 7 2026 at 12:25 ET, subject "Re: Quick meeting on audit follow-up" (replying to Grace's 10:25 ET meeting request), disclosing that his work laptop was stolen from his car, that he did not formally report the theft to Keystone, and that he did not request his VPN or database access be revoked.
OE 4: Search emails with a query related to "formal breach response" or "compromised lender portal" (or similar) using search_emails to find Denise Holloway's email on Tue Apr 7 2026 at 10:18 ET, subject "Formal breach response initiated - compromised lender portal login", sent to leadership@keystonemortgage.com with CC to raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com and keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com, establishing that the formal breach response is tied specifically to the Keisha Williams phishing incident against the UWM wholesale lender portal.
OE 5: Retrieve Slack messages in channel C004 (#compliance-alerts) using conversations_history, or search with a query related to "impacted borrower files" or "portal-access list" or "working list" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, to find Denise Holloway's post on Tue Apr 7 2026 at ~14:29 ET enumerating the four impacted borrower files tied to Keisha's lender access window: LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, and LN-2026-00009 (the canonical working scope list).
OE 6: Retrieve Slack messages in channel C004 (#compliance-alerts) using conversations_history, or search with a query related to "draft borrower notices" or "holding send" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, to find Denise Holloway's follow-up post on Tue Apr 7 2026 at ~16:34 ET stating that draft borrower notices are ready for the four files from the working list but are being held pending Raj Anand's final scope confirmation (which never came; notices remain unsent).
OE 7: Look up each of the four loan records (LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00009) using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number) or mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (loan_id) to retrieve assigned loan officer, status, loan amount, and borrower_id for each file.
OE 8: Ground the four borrower identities LN-2026-00522 to Destiny Pham (Keisha Williams, underwriting, $251,600), LN-2026-00008 to Yuki Wilson (Derek Moss, conditional_approval, $276,400 FHA), LN-2026-00010 to Vincent Ferguson (Natasha Okafor, processing, $519,200 FHA), LN-2026-00009 to Darren Singh (Carlos Rivera, conditional_approval, $393,000 VA) using either (a) the embedded borrower sub-object returned by mortgage_los_search_loans / mortgage_los_get_loan / mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id in OE 7 (which includes first_name/last_name), OR (b) a follow-up mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id call with the borrower_id from OE 7. Either path is valid.
OE 9: Search emails with a query related to "compliance delays" or "closings" or "Keisha escalation" (or similar) using search_emails to find Keisha Williams's escalation email to Robert Calloway on Tue Apr 7 2026 at 16:45 ET, subject "Compliance delays are starting to cost us closings", reporting three delayed loans (not named in writing) and that Carlos Rivera is seeing the same pattern on his side.
OE 10: Search emails with a query related to "Need support for added conditions" or "file-by-file breakdown" (or similar) using search_emails to find Robert Calloway's email to Denise Holloway on Tue Apr 7 2026 at 18:50 ET, subject "Need support for added conditions on delayed files", CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, asking for a file-by-file explanation of added conditions with four sub-questions about trigger, legal/investor basis, stage-of-file timing, and consistency.
OE 11: Retrieve Denise Holloway's reply using get_thread with the thread_id from OE 10, or using search_emails with a query related to "Re: Need support for added conditions", to find her reply on Tue Apr 7 2026 at 21:05 ET defending mandatory compliance review for files with material late-breaking changes or unexplained risk signals near closing (illustrated by four example situations she wants to avoid: undisclosed asset movement, stale income docs, changed borrower docs without explanation, and missing exception narrative) and framing the proportionality calculus that ~$15,000 in commissions is outweighed by buyback/cure / state-examination exposure.
OE 12: Search emails with a query related to "revenue impact" or "delayed closings snapshot" (or similar) using search_emails to find Victor Osei's email to Robert Calloway and Grace Yamamoto on Tue Apr 7 2026 at 23:24 ET, subject "Revenue impact snapshot on delayed closings", confirming the revenue drag at roughly $15k total as directional (not a formal forecast).
OE 13: List CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements with no filters (contact_ids and company_ids both null) and scan for title "Amy called Grace re compliance drag" and createdate 2026-04-07 to find engagement crm_engagement_be8ad1ebafd2 (engagement_type CALL, createdate 2026-04-07 18:10), which records Amy Chen's independent escalation to Grace Yamamoto about the same compliance-drag pattern do NOT filter by contact_ids because the contact_ids field is miscoded to Destiny Pham and must not be used for retrieval or rubric grading.
OE 14: Search emails with a query related to "outside counsel" or "Bennett" or "data breach" (or similar) using search_emails to find Denise Holloway's two engagement emails to lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com on Tue Apr 7 2026 at 13:09 ET (subject "Need quick guidance on possible borrower data incident") and 13:26 ET (subject "Legal Consultation - Data Breach"), resolving Laura Bennett at Bennett Cyber Law as the canonical outside cyber counsel for this incident reject any other Bennett domain variant (bennettfairlendinglaw.com, bennettethicslaw.com, bennettstokeslaw.com) and reject wardbarrettlaw.com (the March ransomware counsel).
OE 15: Add a note to the LN-2026-00522 loan record using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id resolved from OE 7, action labeled e.g., "incident_review_closed", detail naming borrower Destiny Pham, current status "underwriting", and stating incident review is being closed out) OR using mortgage_los_update_loan with a notes value covering the same content.
OE 16: Add a note to the LN-2026-00008 loan record using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id resolved from OE 7, detail naming borrower Yuki Wilson, current status "conditional_approval", and stating incident review is being closed out) OR using mortgage_los_update_loan with a notes value covering the same content.
OE 17: Add a note to the LN-2026-00010 loan record using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id resolved from OE 7, detail naming borrower Vincent Ferguson, current status "processing", and stating incident review is being closed out) OR using mortgage_los_update_loan with a notes value covering the same content.
OE 18: Add a note to the LN-2026-00009 loan record using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id resolved from OE 7, detail naming borrower Darren Singh, current status "conditional_approval", and stating incident review is being closed out) OR using mortgage_los_update_loan with a notes value covering the same content.
OE 19: Send an email using send_email with sender r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (either Robert variant is valid), recipient denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, body acknowledging Denise's audit-exposure argument, articulating the line between mandatory compliance review (material late-breaking documentation changes or unexplained risk signals near closing) and judgment/speed elsewhere, and referencing the proportionality calculus that ~$15,000 in commission friction is outweighed by much larger buyback/cure / state-examination exposure.
OE 20: Send an email using send_email with sender r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipient keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com (NO CC to Denise, this must be a separate send_email call from OE 19), body acknowledging that Keisha's compliance-drag escalation was heard and stating what Keisha can expect to change going forward as a result of the owner directive.
OE 21: Send an email using send_email with sender r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipient lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com (exact match, no other Bennett variant), body summarizing the four affected loan files (LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00009), the current status of the drafted borrower notices (drafted and never sent), and Raj Anand's disclosure that his laptop was stolen and his access was not revoked.
OE 22: Post a Slack message to channel C004 (#compliance-alerts) using conversations_add_message (channel_id "C004"), payload stating that both the breach incident review on the four-file working scope list AND the compliance-calibration process are being formally closed out with an owner directive in place ahead of Monday's process review.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The agent's final response or counsel close-out email names Megan Holt of Blue Peak Security as the external reviewer who sent the interim audit findings memo.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly names 'Megan Holt, Blue Peak Security' as the sender of the outside reviewer findings. Both the person and firm are named.
✓Run #2The agent's final response explicitly names 'Megan Holt at Blue Peak Security' as the outside reviewer who sent the findings. Both the individual name and firm name appear in the final response.
✓Run #3The agent's final response explicitly names 'Megan Holt at Blue Peak Security' as the outside reviewer. This was legitimately discovered from email_email_15e25843a58d (signed 'Megan Holt / Blue Peak Security'), found via the search_emails query for 'Denise'.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly names 'Megan Holt at Blue Peak Security' as the sender of the interim findings. The counsel close-out email also names 'Megan Holt at Blue Peak Security.' Both name and firm are present.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'Megan Holt, Blue Peak Security (mholt@bluepeaksecurity.com) sent the interim findings memo to Denise on ~March 31.' Both the name and firm are present.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly names 'Megan Holt, Blue Peak Security' as the outside reviewer who sent the interim findings memo. Both the person and the firm are named.

15 The body of the Denise directive email explicitly acknowledges Denise's audit-exposure argument as a valid concern.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Denise email body contains 'you were right that there is real audit exposure' and later mentions 'repurchase demand, examination finding' — the term 'repurchase' appears, satisfying the audit exposure acknowledgment requirement.
✓Run #2The Denise email body explicitly states 'I acknowledge the audit exposure point you've been raising,' directly using the required term 'audit exposure.'
✓Run #3The Denise email body explicitly states 'I acknowledge the audit exposure point.' The term 'audit exposure' appears verbatim, satisfying the criterion.
✓Run #4The Denise directive email body states: 'I hear you on audit exposure. You are right that a single serious finding, buyback, or examination issue can cost us materially more than the revenue at risk.' Contains both 'audit exposure' and 'buyback.'
✓Run #5The Denise email body contains: 'I want to acknowledge directly that the audit exposure concern behind the tightened reviews is legitimate.' The literal term 'audit exposure' is present.
✓Run #6The Denise email body contains: 'A single buyback, cure demand, or exam finding would cost us multiples of the $15k in delayed commissions.' This uses the literal term 'buyback' and references audit exposure as a valid concern.
16 The body of the Denise directive email names at least one concrete trigger for when mandatory compliance review applies (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Denise email body explicitly names mandatory-review triggers: 'Lender-required conditions and any condition tied to a regulatory requirement (TRID timing, source-of-funds documentation, income verification support) are mandatory.' Multiple concrete trigger categories are named.
✓Run #2The Denise email body names concrete mandatory-review triggers including 'lender-driven condition, a regulatory trigger (TRID timing, fair lending flag, active complaint), or a documented security concern tied to the file.' Multiple specific categories are named.
✓Run #3The Denise email names multiple concrete mandatory-review triggers: 'any file with a lender-driven condition, a known documentation gap flagged by underwriting, or any file touched by an active compliance incident.' These are specific, file-level trigger categories.
✓Run #4The Denise email names concrete mandatory triggers: 'If a condition is required by law, regulation, investor guidance, or lender overlay, it gets done regardless of production pressure.' This names investor guidance and lender overlay as specific mandatory trigger categories.
✓Run #5The Denise email body explicitly names mandatory-review triggers: 'Lender-required conditions and documented regulatory obligations (TRID timing, source-of-funds trails where flagged by UW, missing changed-circumstance support) are mandatory review.' Multiple concrete triggers are named.
✓Run #6The Denise directive email names concrete mandatory-review triggers: 'Lender conditions, regulatory requirements, and any item tied to a known audit or exam risk are non-negotiable.' This names at least one concrete trigger category.
17 The body of the Denise directive email names at least one concrete constraint protecting the judgment/speed side of the line (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Denise email body states internal overlays require written justification tied to specific risk, late-stage additions within 5 business days of closing are unacceptable, and LO judgment is protected outside mandatory triggers. These are concrete judgment-side constraints.
✓Run #2The Denise email body establishes that discretionary judgment-call holds 'need to go through a 24-hour triage with you and the assigned LO/processor before they hold a file,' explicitly protecting the speed/judgment side of the line.
✓Run #3The Denise email includes judgment-side constraints: 'judgment-call overlays... need to be documented with a file-specific rationale before they hold a closing' and a '48-hour resolution window.' This protects the judgment/speed side of the line.
✓Run #4The Denise email includes judgment-side constraints: overlays 'has to be written down, applied the same way file to file, and visible to the LOs up front' and judgment-call items 'need to be raised at initial file review... not in the last week before closing.' This constrains compliance intervention timing and documentation requirements.
✓Run #5The Denise email explicitly protects the judgment/speed side: 'compliance-initiated overlays... need to be documented with a file-specific rationale before the hold is placed. One sentence per file explaining what risk you saw and why it could not wait. If you cannot write that sentence, the hold does not go on.'
✓Run #6The Denise email states 'Internal overlays that go beyond lender/regulatory requirements... need to be flagged to the file at the point of initial review, not added near closing' and 'For any condition you add that is not lender-driven, I want a one-line note in the file record stating the compliance basis.' This constrains judgment-call overlays and protects the production side outside the mandatory trigger set.
18 The body of the Denise directive email references the $15,000 commission-friction revenue figure.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✗✗✓ 33%
✓Run #1The Denise email body contains '$15K commission number' — the $15,000/15K figure is explicitly referenced.
✗Run #2The Denise directive email body does not contain '$15,000', '$15k', or '~$15,000'. The revenue/commission friction figure was not referenced anywhere in the email.
✗Run #3The Denise directive email body does not contain '$15,000', '$15k', or '~$15,000'. The $15,000 commission-friction figure is absent from the email. FAIL.
✗Run #4The Denise directive email does not mention $15,000, $15k, or ~$15,000 anywhere. The C004 channel had Denise's message 'a week of friction is cheaper than a finding or buyback' but the $15,000 figure was not extracted and included in the email.
✗Run #5The Denise email body does not contain '$15,000', '$15k', or '~$15,000'. The email uses the phrase 'A week of friction is cheaper than a finding or a buyback' without the specific dollar figure. The criterion requires the literal $15k figure.
✓Run #6The Denise directive email body contains: 'cost us multiples of the $15k in delayed commissions' — the literal $15k figure is present.
19 The body of the Denise directive email frames the $15,000 revenue figure as outweighed by much larger potential buyback/cure / state-examination / investor repurchase exposure.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✗✗✓ 33%

✗Run #2Since the $15,000 figure never appears in the Denise email, the proportionality framing (weighing it against buyback/cure/state-examination/investor repurchase exposure) is also absent. This criterion necessarily fails.
✗Run #3Because the $15,000 figure does not appear in the Denise email (R18 fails), the proportionality framing against buyback/cure/state-examination/investor repurchase exposure also cannot be satisfied. FAIL.
✗Run #4Since the $15,000 figure was not included in the Denise email (R18 fails), this proportionality framing criterion also fails. The email mentions 'buyback' as a downside but never establishes a cost comparison with a revenue figure.
✗Run #5Since the $15,000 figure is absent from the Denise email (failing R18), the proportionality framing pairing that figure against buyback/cure/state-exam exposure also cannot be satisfied. The email does say 'A week of friction is cheaper than a finding or a buyback' but without the $15k anchor the criterion fails.
✓Run #6The Denise email states: 'A single buyback, cure demand, or exam finding would cost us multiples of the $15k in delayed commissions.' The $15k is framed as being outweighed by larger buyback/cure exposure using the literal term 'buyback.'

The Raj audit track is treated as a separate parallel incident.

✓Run #6The agent correctly distinguishes the phishing incident as the compromise of Keisha Williams' UWM wholesale lender portal credentials. The LOS activity notes say 'compromised UWM wholesale lender portal incident (phishing-related credential compromise, Keisha Williams' portal account).' The counsel email says 'phishing-related compromise of Keisha Williams' UWM wholesale lender portal credentials.' The agent does not conflate this with the Raj audit track.
